diagrams.title: Steganography Process Flow
diagrams.description: Shows the process of embedding secret data into a cover object to create a stego-object
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes how steganography hides data within cover media, showing the encoding and decoding process flow.

diagrams.title: LSB Bit Replacement Example
diagrams.description: Illustrates how LSB steganography modifies individual bits in pixel color values
elements: Green value: 200 (11001000) modified to 200 (11001000) - no change for illustration
Blue value: 178 (10110010) modified to 179 (10110011) - LSB changed
Visual difference: 0.4% - imperceptible to human eye
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how small changes in least significant bits are visually imperceptible while carrying hidden data.

diagrams.title: Steganography vs Cryptography Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing the fundamental difference between cryptography and steganography
elements: Cryptography: 'X7#k9@m2#p' (encrypted, but you know something is hidden)
Steganography: Looks like normal text/image, but contains hidden 'ATTACK AT DAWN'
Key difference: One shows but scrambles; the other hides existence entirely
diagrams.purpose: Clearly illustrates the conceptual difference: cryptography protects content, steganography hides the very fact of communication.
